name: Cashew-Chicken with Broccoli
recipeInstructions:
1. Blanch broccoli in boiling water for 2 minutes; rinse in cold water; drain; set aside.
2. Coat a skillet with cooking spray. Over medium heat, sauté onion, garlic, and bell pepper for about 10 minutes. Remove from pan and set aside, keeping warm.
3. Wipe the skillet with a paper towel and coat very lightly with cooking spray. Add cubed chicken and sauté over medium heat until golden brown, about 8 minutes.
4. Add soy sauce, 3/4 cup chicken broth, and reserved onion mixture to the pan. Arrange blanched broccoli on top. Simmer, covered, for 5 minutes. Remove broccoli with a slotted spoon and keep warm.
5. Dissolve cornstarch in the remaining 1/2 cup broth. Add ground red pepper and black pepper (if using). Stir into the chicken mixture and simmer until thickened. Add bean sprouts and simmer for 1 minute.
6. Using a slotted spoon, transfer the chicken-bean sprout mixture to a warmed serving plate. Top with reserved warm broccoli and drizzle with sauce. Sprinkle with cashews. Serve over rice.
per serving (without rice): 190 Kcal, 5.5g fat (1.1g sat fat), 716mg Na
